flavor: "The Empress declared a great celebration to mark the victory, and the Champions of the Great Clans were honored with invitations to her court." - The Chronicles of Empress Iweko, Part V
text: Discard zero to two cards; each other player in turn order may discard a card. Each player then draws as many cards as he discarded.

title: A New Year
flavor: With each new year come new challenges and celebrations, but few years see the ascension of a new Emperor. When it occurs, a series of twenty festivals is held to commemorate the occasion.
text: Open: Discard zero to two cards; each other player in turn order may discard a card. Each player then draws as many cards as he discarded.
Open: Discard zero to two cards; each other player in turn order may discard a card. Each player then draws as many cards as he discarded.
title: A New Year

flavor: "You have come to take me to the islands, then?" "No my lady," Okame replied. "We have come to defend your home."
text: Until the end of the game, after any action resolves that produced a Ranged Attack, if any target of the Ranged Attack is still in play, straighten any cards that performed the action.

notes: Legal July 1st, 2015 • Thunderous Acclaim Pre-release Promo
title: A Prophet Revealed
flavor: "All over the Empire, there are others now experiencing the same visions. But only you have had this gift for so long, and at last, I have found you, Kitsune Narako." - Hira, formerly Asahina Hira, Keeper of the Void
text:
After your action resolves, if any targets of its Ranged Attacks are still in play, straighten any of your cards that the action and combining abilities bowed.
Open: Put this Event into play.

After your action resolves, if any targets of its Ranged Attacks are still in play, straighten any of your cards that the action and combining abilities bowed.
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: A Prophet Revealed

flavor: "From east to west the samurai of the Empire girded themselves for war, and answered the Empress's call to do great deeds in her name. The skies were filled with the banners of the Great Clans." - The Chronicles of Empress Iweko, Part II
text: Each player with more than 20 Family Honor loses 5 Honor.

flavor: "Hachi and the Anvil had come to an agreement at last. Ironically, it was then that the Horde attacked Otosan Uchi."
"Because the battle has ended, you think that we should grow comfortable? There is never a better time for action than when it is not yet needed. Now is the time for preparation."
text: Open: Each player with 21 or more Family Honor loses 5 Honor.
title: A Time for Action

text: Open: Each player with 21 or more Family Honor loses 5 Honor.
title: A Time for Action

flavor: "This land is fraught with peril, and not only the physical. Our souls must remain pure." - Yung

text: Until the end of the game, any player whose Dynasty Deck is depleted and cannot refill his or her Provinces must destroy one of his or her Personalities or holdings in play for each Province left empty at the end of that player's turn.

After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until the game ends, before each player's turn ends, if his Dynasty deck has no cards, he chooses and destroys one Personality or Holding he controls for each of his Provinces without a Dynasty card in it.
title: Abandoning the Fortunes

flavor: When Naseru brokered the alliance between the Lion and the Phoenix, his plan was for Lion might and Isawa magic to smash the Shadowlands once and for all. Unfortunately, the Dragon refugees crossing both Lion and Phoenix borders only further unified the two clans against a common threat.
text: Select a Faction other than your own. For the rest of the game, you can bring Personalities of that Faction into play for 2 less Gold.

flavor: "Any man who does not consider me an ally is an enemy." - Hida Kisada
Two men can brave what one man cannot.
Two men can brave what one man cannot.
"Our clans have shared both friendship and enmity. We must now ask ourselves, which one brought greater benefit to us both?"
text:
You do not pay the extra two Gold for bringing Personalities with the named Clan Alignment into play.
Open: Put this Event into play. Name a Clan Alignment other than your own.
title: Alliance

text:
You do not pay the extra two Gold for bringing Personalities with the named Clan Alignment into play.
Open: Put this Event into play. Name a Clan Alignment other than your own.
title: Alliance

flavor: "Ambitious men might succumb to temptation. Our duty is to record without bias. We alone can do so." - Miya Shoin
"The winners choose what the histories will say," Ikoma Hagio said softly. "And we shall ensure that history remembers the true heroes of this war."

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, after the next time this game you destroy a Province as the Attacker, the Defender loses 5 Honor.
title: Alter History

flavor: "The air was filled with flames, smoke, battle cries, the screams of the dying. It was glorious. Few escaped our righteous blades." - Yamazaki

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your turn two turns from now begins, all players have the ability, "Battle: Target your performing unbowed attacking Personality and bow all unbowed cards in his unit to destroy a target enemy card without attachments."
title: Assault in the Jungle

flavor: Iweko Setai often represents his wife in court.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, each player in turn order starting with you may choose a card in his hand. Discard all chosen cards. If exactly one player's card has the lowest Focus Value of all those cards, he gains a Province to the left of his leftmost province. If exactly one player's card has the highest Focus Value of all those cards, permanently give him +3 maximum hand size, and he draws three cards.
title: Benefices of the Emperor

flavor: "You will die waiting for us to surrender, dogs!"

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, if any other player controls a Personality, then at the start of your next turn, if this Province still exists, permanently give it +3 strength and gain 3 Honor, and until then, after battle resolution destroys this Province, give each Personality in the attacking army a +1F token and the Attacker may draw a card.
title: Boastful Proclamation

flavor: "Mara's body was not recovered from the ruins."

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, any player who controls the Imperial Favor discards it, until your turn two turns from now begins, actions that discard the Imperial Favor as a condition upon further effects have their effects negated if they target a Nonhuman Personality, and until the game ends, Nonhumans may not perform Political actions.
title: Burning Dreams

flavor: "Rokugan is a land of festivals. It is necessary for the common people to have release from the tedium of their honorable labors. They serve the Empress loyally, and in return she offers them grand celebrations." - Voice of the Emperor

Blown in from the west,
Fallen petals gathering.
To the east, they fall.
- Rezan the Ronin Poet

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, no players may take Reactions until your next turn begins.
title: Cherry Blossom Festival

text: Before a player's turn ends this game, if the player, during that turn, destroyed a province in battle resolution, put at least two Rings into play by their own text, and gained 7 or more Honor, he may, as costs, discard the Imperial Favor and pay 10 Gold. If he does so, he wins the game.

flavor: Who shall be worthy?

notes: Legal April 1st, 2015 • Twenty Festivals Pre-release Promo
text:
Players have the ability, "Dynasty: If you, this turn, destroyed a Province in battle resolution, put two Rings into play by their own text, and gained 7 Honor, you may discard the Imperial Favor and pay 10 Gold to win the game."
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Claiming the Throne

text:
Players have the ability, "Dynasty: If you, this turn, destroyed a Province in battle resolution, put two Rings into play by their own text, and gained 7 Honor, you may discard the Imperial Favor and pay 10 Gold to win the game."
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Claiming the Throne

flavor: "I see the festivities began without me. Just as well, I suppose. You would need a short practice to have any chance against me."
"It is unfortunate that you were delayed, my lord, but regrettably we cannot admit you until the Empress' court is concluded."

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them,, choose a Personality in play. He may not be assigned to attack you until your next turn begins.
title: Delayed Arrival

flavor: "Your actions have brought shame to your entire family. Others will shoulder a share of your burden until such time as your disgrace can be forgotten."

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your turn two turns from now begins, other players may not target your Personalities with Limited actions.
title: Disgrace

text: Until you are eliminated, all players draw an additional Fate card during their End Phases, and each time a player needs to draw a Fate card but cannot because his Fate deck is empty, destroy his rightmost Province.

flavor: "Defeated and cast out from his city, the Dark Lord would nevertheless return stronger than ever." - Chronicle of the Spider Clan

notes: Legal January 1st, 2016 • The Blackest Storm Pre-release Promo
title: Doom of the Dark Lord
flavor: The soul of Goju Kyoden has returned from death yet again. But does this bode ill for the enemies of Kanpeki, or for the son of Daigotsu himself?
text:
Each player draws an additional card at the end of his turn. After each time a player must draw a card but cannot because his deck is empty, destroy his rightmost Province.
Open: Put this Event into play.

Each player draws an additional card at the end of his turn. After each time a player must draw a card but cannot because his deck is empty, destroy his rightmost Province.
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Doom of the Dark Lord

flavor: The lords of the Empire gather weapons and men, preparing for the conflicts they can see on the horizon.
text: Until the game ends, during each player's End Phase, he may draw an additional card if he brought any Followers into play from his hand that turn.

flavor: "Your life is owed to the Emperor, peasant. Serve him, or I will collect that debt."
Your place is not in the fields of rice, but fields of battle!
"To arms, you filthy dogs! The time has come to teach the Scorpion a lesson!"

title: Enlistment
flavor: Even among the loyal people of Rokugan, there are some who do not wish to serve unless forced.
text:
Each player may draw an additional card before his turn ends if he Equipped any Followers from his hand since his last turn ended.
Open: Put this Event into play.

Each player may draw an additional card before his turn ends if he Equipped any Followers from his hand since his last turn ended.
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Enlistment

designernotes: Legal July 1

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your next turn begins, before the first time each turn another player's card's effect destroys one or more of your Personalities, negate the destruction.
title: Expansion

flavor: "Five magistrates died! An entire district was burned to the ground!" "Yes, Shiba-san, and we shall address the consequences in time. Please, let us discuss how we might make amends…"

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, after the next time this game your Honor is below its starting value, you have a tea ceremony; gain 5 Honor.
title: Formal Apology

flavor: The Crane sought clear dominance with every small victory.
designernotes: [Art Notes: A typical scene at winter court, involving a Scorpion courtier.]

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, you may search your Fate deck for a non-Unique Strategy with a Political ability, show it, put it in your hand, and discard a card.
title: Gaining Advantage

notes: Some variants of the Pearl Edition version of this card were printed with a different text alignment.

flavor: Three hundred years ago, a sandalwood fan convinced the Court that the Unicorn Clan was indeed Shinjo's people returned from their quest.
text: Draw an extra card during your End Phase this turn.

flavor: She was a sign of good luck, foretelling fortune in our future.
She was a sign of good luck, foretelling fortune in our future.
She was a sign of good luck, foretelling fortune in our future.
She was a sign of good luck, foretelling fortune in our future.
Fortune favors the bold.
Fortune favors the bold.
"All things are necessary - war, peace, the edge of the sword, and the sound of a poem. With understanding, all things are one." - from the True Tao of Shinsei
Three hundred years ago, a sandalwood fan convinced the Court that the Unicorn Clan was indeed Shinjo's people returned from their quest.
The ki-rin is an ancient and sacred creature of legend. It appears in the mortal realm perhaps once each generation, and those who see it are blessed.
text: Dynasty: Draw a card. (You may only take Dynasty actions during your Dynasty Phase.)
title: Glimpse of the Unicorn

text: Dynasty: Draw a card. (You may only take Dynasty actions during your Dynasty Phase.)
title: Glimpse of the Unicorn

flavor: "In times when the Emperor cannot defend the Empire, the Shogun must do so in his place." - Sun Tao
The Shogun is dead, but many of his officers are ready to bring glory to his memory.
"Kaneka is the first Shogun. It falls to him to define that title's role and purpose. I pity an who would impede his pursuit of that purpose." - Kakita Munemori

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until the game ends, Equipping is a Battle/Open action (for all players), and after the first time each turn each player Equips a card with a printed Gold Cost of 5 or more from his hand, that player may draw a card.
title: Glory of the Shogun

flavor: The Spider were the victims, but they were not the only ones to suffer.
designernotes: A burning fortress.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, destroy all non-Unique Holdings with a printed Gold Cost of 0 and, until the game ends, Holdings in and out of play have a minimum Gold Cost of 1.
title: Growing Hostility

flavor: "Take what we are owed. Leave the rest for the crows." - Kakita Idzuki

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, you may destroy this Province to draw three cards.
title: Harsh Choices

flavor: "I bear a gift for you, commander, to commemorate your service and glorious victory for the Empress."
"What weapon is stronger than a gift?" - Doji Tanitsu
Katsutoshi was looking for the best time to present the Empress with the Sword of the Hantei.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, gain 2 Honor and search your Fate deck for an Item, show it, and put it into your hand.
title: Imperial Gift

flavor: "When seeking a teacher, first find those who know what you know, then those who know what you do not." - The Tao of Shinsei
designernotes: [Note: This is someone searching for a Sensei.]

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, search your Dynasty deck for a Sensei Personality. Refill this Province with him, face-up.
title: In Search of Guidance

flavor: "Your efforts are a great boon to the alliance, noble warrior. You honor us all with your prowess."
text: Until the game ends, after each time a player destroys a province by winning a battle, he gains 3 Honor.

flavor: "The Lion respect the Phoenix, my lord, but your intervention in this matter is unwelcome. Stand aside, or our alliance will suffer for it."
"I bring you a gift, the head of the man who shamed both our houses. Perhaps now we can grow in strength together once again."
text:
After each time an Attacker destroys a province by winning a battle, he gains 3 Honor.
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Military Alliance

text:
After each time an Attacker destroys a province by winning a battle, he gains 3 Honor.
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Military Alliance

flavor: "Just take your filthy money and go." "There is no reason to be hostile. One could imagine how much every koku helps the Scorpion defend the southern borders."

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose another player's Personality in play. After the next time this game his unit is assigned or an action resolves that he performed, dishonor him, and his controller loses 2 Honor.
title: Minor Blackmail

flavor: "Onnotangu and Amaterasu gave way to Hitomi and Yakamo, who gave way to the Obsidian Moon and the Jade Sun. The Heavens are complete and their blessings are showered upon the mortal realm." - Matsu Kasei
"The blood of Lord Sun flows through the Crab, and Lady Moon protects the Dragon." - Naka Tokei

"…And ever after, the Moon and the Sun circled in the heavens, always seeking to finish their final duel. The cycle of Atman has begun again, and again the world has found peace." - from Fall and Rebirth, Part VI

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, each player may shuffle an Event in his discard pile into his deck.
title: Moon and Sun

flavor: "Naoharu is long since gone, but his lessons endure for a new generation to learn." - Doji Shunya
Every gift comes with a price.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, show a card in your hand to another player. You offer it as a gift. He may put it in his hand; if he does, you gain Honor equal to its printed Focus Value. If you showed a card and he does not put it in his hand, discard the card and draw two cards.
title: Naoharu's Gift

flavor: "Reiha-sama, a unit of men has appeared, and they bear the Spider mon." "Do they have oni or goblins with them? If not, put them on the left flank. The line is getting thin."

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your turn three turns from now begins, you may target Followers in your discard pile with the Equip rulebook action.
title: Never Enough Soldiers

flavor: The stresses of the Destroyer War drove many clans to offer fealty to ronin, something they had never before done.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your turn two turns from now begins, after each time an Unaligned Human Personality turns face-up in one of your Provinces, permanently give him your Clan Alignment. He has sworn fealty to you.
notes: Released in the 2011 Summer Con pack
title: Open Recruitment

flavor: "I cannot promise that it shall never fall again. But the Crab shall always be here to rebuild it." - Hida Kisada

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your second turn from now begins, the Attacker may not announce a Battle action before the Defender's first normal opportunity to act or pass in a battle.
title: Rebuilding Kyuden Hida

flavor: The end of an era, the beginning of another.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, any player with three or fewer Provinces may gain a Province to the right of his rightmost Province. Each player who gained a Province from this has his Family Honor lowered to its starting value, and he may not declare an attack until your next turn begins. This Honor loss will not be negated or given to another player instead.
title: Rebuilding the Empire

flavor: One by one, each star in the Celestial Heavens awakened, following the light of a golden colt.
text: Each Nonhuman Personality gains +1F/+1C. Each Nonhuman Follower gains +1F.

flavor: "Tales of spirits, wondrous events, even sightings of the elemental dragons." The Phoenix shugenja's eyes were alight. "The Heavens are clear in their blessings of the Iweko Dynasty."
text: Open: Give each Nonhuman Personality +1F/+1C and give each Nonhuman Follower +1F.
title: Return of Myth

text: Open: Give each Nonhuman Personality +1F/+1C and give each Nonhuman Follower +1F.
title: Return of Myth

designernotes: Legal October 1st, 2012

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, the cultists of Ruhmal are restless; until your next turn begins, after each time a card effect destroys a Personality, the player who last controlled him discards a card.
notes: Released to the 2012 Quarter 3 Organized Play winners
title: Ritual of Sacrifice

flavor: Foreigners are seldom welcomed in the Empire.
text:
Discard the Imperial Favor if you control it.
Until the game ends, you cannot win an Honor
Victory, lobby, or control the Imperial Favor, and you
have the ability, "Reaction: Any number of times per
turn, after an action with a base cost of discarding
the Favor targets one of your cards: Lose 5 Honor.
Negate the action's effects."

flavor: "The Colonies have their purpose, certainly, but respectable? There are gaijin living there." - Bayushi Nitoshi
text:
You cannot win an Honor Victory or control the Imperial Favor. You have the ability, "Repeatable Interrupt: Negate a Favor action after it targets your cards (the Imperial Favor is still discarded). Lose 5 Honor."
Open: Put this Event into play. Discard the Imperial Favor if you control it.
title: Severed from the Emperor

text:
You cannot win an Honor Victory or control the Imperial Favor. You have the ability, "Repeatable Interrupt: Negate a Favor action after it targets your cards (the Imperial Favor is still discarded). Lose 5 Honor."
Open: Put this Event into play. Discard the Imperial Favor if you control it.
title: Severed from the Emperor

flavor: "The Lost are slaves to corruption, mere pawns of the power of Fu Leng. Powerful Bloodspeakers such as Iuchiban and myself have learned to use outside magic to control the Taint. We are the true masters of darkness." - Yajinden's Journals
text: Permanently lower the strength of each Province by 1.

flavor: "The gibbering things marched openly through the streets, wearing the colors of the Hantei. None dared show themselves for fear of joining their ranks." - The Fall of the Hantei, Volume I

notes: Legal October 1st, 2015 • Evil Portents Pre-release Promo
title: Shadow of the Dark God
flavor: Shadows grow ever longer as the sun sets... and, some say, this shadow bears a pair of horns.
text:
Provinces have -1 strength.
Open: Put this Event into play.

Provinces have -1 strength.
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Shadow of the Dark God

flavor: "It seems as if winter comes sooner every year." - Satoshi
text: Until the beginning of your next turn, each player may take only one Limited or Open action during each Action Phase. (This does not affect battles.)

flavor: The winter season was short and quite mild, beginning with the Empress's announcement and ending with a coronation.
text:
Each player may take only one Limited or Open action per turn (after this Event enters play).
Open: If you have not taken an action this phase, put this Event into play. Discard it after the current active player's next Action Phase begins.
title: Short Season

text:
Each player may take only one Limited or Open action per turn (after this Event enters play).

title: Short Season

flavor: "It is a marvel," Tsukimi said. "A painfully costly marvel," Bairei replied. "But it will allow us to contact the Mantis."

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, look at the top four cards of any Fate deck. You may discard one of them. Put the rest back in any order.
title: Signs and Portents

flavor: "Good day, Champion. I am pleased to surrender this fugitive to you for justice. I believe there is a stipend for his capture?"
There are few who can escape a Tsuruchi magistrate's long reach. In the end, all criminals must face their fate.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your turn three turns from now begins, after each time a Personality opposing a Magistrate or Courtier becomes dishonorable from an action, the Personality's controller loses 2 Honor.
title: Successful Bounty

flavor: "The greatest dramas always leave the matter of guilt or innocence slightly ambivalent. Is it any surprise the Scorpion feature prominently in so many?" - Bayushi Hurunayi
Beware lest you lose the substance by grasping at shadows.
"Utagu's death is a tragedy for Rokugan and his clan. For those of who truly revere the kami, his death is a lesson, one which must be heeded if the future of Rokugan is to flourish." - Isawa Taeruko

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until the game ends, before each of your Action Phases begins, each player in turn order starting with you loses 1 Honor.
title: Suspicions

flavor: "Finally, the Destroyer and her elite took to the battlefield. Some hearts shattered, unable to bear her presence. Others were kindled to consuming rage." - Chronicles of the Bitter Spring
"Finally, the Destroyer and her elite took to the battlefield. Some hearts shattered, unable to bear her presence. Others were kindled to consuming rage." - Chronicles of the Bitter Spring

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your turn two turns from now begins, after the resolution of any Battle action, give each of your Nonhumans who performed it a +1F Frenzy token.
title: The Agony of Her Gaze

flavor: "Go and bring the Imperial presence to the lesser courts." - Mandate of the Amethyst Champion

flavor: To follow in the footsteps of the legendary Kuni Daigo is a burden covered by many.

title: The Jade Championship

flavor: The Emerald Legions moved throughout the Empire, ensuring that the Empress's commands were obeyed. Foolish was the lord who tried to defy a legion commander.
text: You may destroy your rightmost Province; if you do so, permanently increase the amount of Honor needed to achieve an Honor Victory by 10.

flavor: "The time has come for the Empire to be reunited," Tsai said to the Legionnaires. "All will bow. Either by choice or before the Emperor's sword."

title: The New Order
flavor: Whenever power changes hands, there are those who seek opportunity.

flavor: "Stand firm and let us see which of us is worthy of service." - Daigotsu Hotako
designernotes: Legal January 1st, 2012

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Personalities in play. Permanently give him +1F, Shadowlands, and the ability, "Battle: Target an enemy card with lower Force. Bow it. Destroy it if it is an attachment."
notes: Released to the 2011 Obsidian Championship participants
title: The Obsidian Championship

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Shugenja in play. Permanently give him Shadowlands and the ability, "Maho Limited, :bow:: Destroy your target Personality to search your discard pile, then deck, then Provinces for an Oni Personality, Recruit it (paying all costs), and lose 3 Honor."

After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Shugenja in play. Permanently give him Shadowlands and the ability, "Maho Limited, :bow:: Destroy your other target Personality to search your discard pile, then deck, then Provinces for an Oni Personality, Recruit it (paying all costs), and lose 3 Honor."

After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Shugenja in play. Permanently give him Shadowlands and the ability, "Maho Limited, :bow:: Destroy your target Personality to search your discard pile, then deck, then Provinces for an Oni Personality, Recruit it (paying all costs), and lose 3 Honor."

flavor: "Stand aside or die, fools!" - Takasho
designernotes: Legal January 1st, 2012

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Shugenja in play. Permanently give him Shadowlands and the ability, "Maho Limited, :bow:: Destroy your other target Personality to search your discard pile, then deck, then Provinces for an Oni Personality, Recruit it (paying all costs), and lose 3 Honor."
notes: Released to the 2011 Only Championship participants

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Personalities in play. Permanently give him Imperial, Magistrate, and the ability, "Battle: Bow a target enemy Personality; if he is dishonorable, destroy a card without attachments in his unit."

Choose one of your Personalities in play. Permanently give him Imperial • Magistrate and the ability, "Battle: Target an enemy Personality: Bow him. If he is dishonorable, destroy a card without attachments in his unit."

flavor: "I served the dojo with honor to the best of my abilities. Do the same, and stand tall." - Yasuki Miliko
designernotes: Legal January 1st, 2012

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Personalities in play. Permanently give him Imperial, Magistrate, and the ability, "Battle: Bow a target enemy Personality; if he is dishonorable, destroy a card without attachments in his unit."
notes: Released to the 2011 Ruby Championship participants
title: The Ruby Championship

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until the game ends, other players' actions will not discard cards from your hand, and if a player has resolved two actions that put cards in his hand in one phase, no more cards will be put into his hand that phase.

Until the game ends, other players' actions will not discard cards from your hand, and if a player has resolved two actions that put cards in his hand in one phase, no more cards will be put into his hand that phase.

flavor: The Scorpion readily accepted the aid of the Crab - together they held a dark secret.
designernotes: The Wall being built around the Second Shadowlands is finally complete.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until the game ends, other players' actions will not discard cards from your hand, and if a player has resolved two actions that put cards in his hand in one phase, no more cards will be put into his hand that phase.
erratum: The word "after" changed to "if" via technical erratum.
title: The Scorpion Wall is Finished

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Personalities in play without abilities. Permanently give him +1F/+1C, the trait "Before the first time each turn another player's card effect destroys this Personality, negate the destruction", and the ability, "Battle: Destroy a target enemy card without attachments."

Choose one of your Personalities in play without abilities. Permanently give him +1F/+1C, the trait "Before the first time each turn another player's card effect destroys this Personality: Negate the destruction", and the ability, "Battle: Target an enemy card without attachments: Destroy it."

flavor: The Empire's finest gather in Tsuma every year...
designernotes: Legal January 1st, 2012

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Personalities in play without abilities. Permanently give him +1F/+1C, the trait "Before the first time each turn another player's card effect destroys this Personality, negate the destruction", and the ability, "Battle: Destroy a target enemy card without attachments."
notes: Released to the 2011 Topaz Championship participants
title: The Topaz Championship

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Personalities in play. Permanently give him Artisan and the ability, "Limited: Choose a player. He gains or loses 1 Honor."
Choose one of your Personalities in play. Permanently give him Artisan and the ability, "Limited: Choose a player. He gains or loses 1 Honor."

flavor: "The arts are as important as steel or prayers. They sustain and uplift us when out spirits are at their lowest point." - Doji Rengetsu
designernotes: Legal January 1st, 2012

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose one of your Personalities in play. Permanently give him Artisan and the ability, "Limited: Choose a player. He gains or loses 1 Honor."
notes: Released to the 2011 Turquoise Championship participants
title: The Turquoise Championship

flavor: With the ranks of the Great Clans depleted, it was difficult to enforce the Empress' laws.

text: Until your second turn from now begins, all provinces have a maximum strength equal to their base strength, and after each time another player who controls no units at the current battlefield announces a Battle action, he discards a card.
title: Times of Strife

flavor: "Toson, I know the commander of that legion, and he is inept. Dispose of him so that Shigetoshi can put someone competent in his place." "Your will, Lady Miyoko."

text: You may choose and destroy a Region. Until your next turn begins, negate all movement to and from battlefields.
title: Too Close to Home

flavor: By the end of the Destroyer War, many had perished, much land went fallow, and even samurai learned the despair that comes with months of hunger.

text: Until your second turn from now begins, after the resolution of the first action each phase that one or more Personalities performed, bow them.
title: War Weariness

flavor: "The people of the Empire, willing to uproot themselves and move across the world to enact the will of the Empress, are blessed among all mortal people." - The Voice of the Empress

text: After your next turn begins, permanently give each of your provinces +2 strength.
story: To all of our volunteers who make this game great.
title: Willing Spirits

flavor: "In twenty years of relative peace and exploration, we have learned so much. We must be ever vigilant to ensure that wisdom is not forsaken for war." - Shiba Tsukimi
<i>"It may take some time to gain admission to the Empress," Taiko said. "We should learn as much as we can in the meantime." Hideo nodded. "Agreed, Little Hummingbird."

"I saw terrible things within the Shadowlands, my lord, but I cannot speak of it to you, or it could bring ruination to you, and to the clan. If for this you wish my seppuku, I will gladly grant it." - Akodo Shunori
"The Brothers of Shinsei are the custodians of the prophet's wisdom." - Togashi Mitsu
It was the wisdom of a simple man that saved an Empire. The Keepers are sworn to uphold his legacy.
"Remember. Think. Feel. Act. Simple things upon which the fate of the Empire turns." - Mirumoto Masae
It was the wisdom of a simple man that saved an Empire. The Keepers are sworn to uphold his legacy.
"There is little wisdom to be gained in the keeping of secrets, brother Lion. It was the Scorpion who took the papers… and who laid the blame at your feet."

"I saw terrible things within the Shadowlands, my lord, but I cannot speak of it to you, or it could bring ruination to you, and to the clan. If for this you wish my seppuku, I will gladly grant it." - Akodo Shunori
The only truth you find in distant lands is the truth you carry with you.
Some seek the wisdom of the universe within monasteries, severing themselves from all outside influences. Others immerse themselves in the natural world in hopes of becoming one with it.
text: Open: Starting with you, each player may search his discard pile and Fate deck for a Ring, show it, and put it in his hand.
title: Wisdom Gained

text: Starting with you, each player may search his discard pile and Fate deck for a Ring, show it, and put it in his hand.
title: Wisdom Gained

text: Open: Starting with you, each player may search his discard pile and Fate deck for a Ring, show it, and put it in his hand.
title: Wisdom Gained

flavor: In the jungle depths, the Spider uncovered the Temple of Ruhmal. In the jungle depths, it was waiting for them.
text: Search your Fate deck or discard pile for a card titled Blood of the Preserver, show it, and put it into your hand.
designernotes: A group of Spider explorers entering the Temple of Kali-Ma and discovering the Blood of the Destroyer.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, search your Fate deck or discard pile for a card titled Blood of the Preserver, show it, and put it into your hand.
title: Discovering the Temple

flavor: As the colonies spread, events unfolding at the heart of the Empire seemed to matter less and less.
text: Until your third turn from now ends, you have the trait, "In your Dynasty Phase, one time for each of your provinces destroyed this game, you may refill one of your provinces face-up."
designernotes: Claim a Province and develop it.  It's vulnerable to attack when you first move in and it is slow to produce

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your third turn from now ends, you have the trait, "In your Dynasty Phase, one time for each of your Provinces destroyed this game, you may refill one of your Provinces face-up."
title: Distant Expansion

flavor: On is the face one presents to all others in Rokugan. To allow this to slip, to show emotion or otherwise act inappropriately, is a great dishonor.
text: Until the game ends, at the end of each phase, lower each player's Honor to what it was at the start of that phase plus 3 plus any Honor he gained from battle resolution that phase; this Honor loss will not be negated, substituted or reduced.
designernotes: Dishonor or dishonor-meta; while honor is internal, "face" is not, and one can lose face by witnessing dishonorable behavior as well as by participating; Art Notes: others turn away ashamed of two men arguing

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until the game ends, at the end of phase, lower each player's Honor to what it was at the start of that phase plus 3 plus any Honor he gained from battle resolution that phase; this Honor loss will not be negated, substituted, or reduced.
title: On

flavor: She thought their happiness would bring two clans together. Instead, his pride tore them apart.
text: Choose two Personalities controlled by the same player. Until the game ends, they may not be assigned, and will not move, to each other's battlefields.
designernotes: Shikei refusing to marry Naleesh.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose two Personalities controlled by the same player. Until the game ends, they may not be assigned, and will not move, to each other's battlefields.
title: Star-Crossed Lovers

flavor: Even in times of peace, a samurai's duty is to prepare for war.
text: Until the game ends, before the second or later time each turn another player's card's effect bows any of your Personalities during the Action Phase, negate that effect, and before the second or later time each turn another player's card's effect prevents any of your Personalities from assigning, negate that effect.
designernotes: [Art Note: With clan feuding resuming, this shows a Personality ignoring whatever is holding him back and prepping for war.]

text: Until the game ends, before the second or later time each turn another player's card's effect bows any of your Personalities during the Action Phase, negate that effect, and before the second or later time each turn another player's card's effect prevents any of your Personalities from assigning, negate that effect.
title: Wartime Preparations

flavor: Over the course of decades, the quiet intensity and visceral starkness of the Turquoise Champion's poems gained an incredible following among the people of Rokugan.
text: After your next End Phase begins, if you did not put any cards in your hand since the resolution of this Event, draw two cards.
designernotes: Legal December 1, 2012

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, after your next Dynasty Phase ends, if you did not put any cards in your hand since the resolution of this Event, draw two cards.
notes: Released in the 2012 Summer Con pack
title: Tales of Valor

flavor: "What an interesting effect this item has." - Daigotsu Kanpeki
designernotes: That's probably too long, but my idea is an Event that acts like The Blasted Lands until the land recovers (ie, you get a Region).  It hoses the Province all around since it has a get-out clause (for some decks).

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, choose a Province. Permanently give it -2 strength and destroy any Region attached to it. Until the game ends, after a Region attaches to it, destroy the Region, and after a Holding enters play from it, give the Holding a -1GP token.
title: Dark Experiments

flavor: The changes in climate, the slight difference in diet, and a dozen other things made the horses skittish. When the wildfire broke out, many were already so anxious that they could not recover from it.
designernotes: Piles of dead horses.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your second turn from now begins, Personalities may not be targeted by the rulebook Cavalry player ability.
title: Incredible Tragedy

flavor: "Even peasants honor their ancestors. No samurai should ever neglect these rituals." - Kitsu Kagako
designernotes: A group of peasants celebrating a festival for the dead.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, for each dead Personality in your discard pile, create a 2F/2C/3PH Samurai • Ancestor • Spirit Personality with your Clan Alignment. If this created no Personalities, you may shuffle this Event into your deck instead of discarding it normally.
title: Offering Reverence

flavor: "Other monks talk to hear themselves talking. Mukudori-sensei says only what needs to be said." - Hiruma Sawai
designernotes: [Art Note: This shows Fudo's path to finding enlightenment, which is easier but not the true path (You play Rings easily, but they don't count towards enlightenment)]

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your second turn from now begins, you have the ability, "Limited: Put a target Ring in your hand into play. While it remains in play, it does not count towards an Enlightenment Victory."
title: Starting on the Path

flavor: "The Crane are masters of diplomacy, but have encountered few foes as recalcitrant as the Mantis. Still, in the aftermath of the battle, even the Mantis desired peace." - Asako Kikugoro
designernotes: [Note: The Crane-Mantis war ends.]

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until your next turn begins, after each time a player creates an Attack Phase, destroy his rightmost Province.
title: Diplomacy Talks

flavor: He reached for the results with a trembling hand. If the Moon Die showed Void, his entire fortune was lost.
designernotes: Bandits playing a game of dice.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, you may discard your hand (even if it is empty) to draw four cards.
title: A Dangerous Game

flavor: "How could they march against us?" "I do not know, but the Second City must know that the Legions are on the way!"
use: Should have something to do with the Lion warning the Second City that the two Imperial legions are coming to the Second City
designernotes: [Note: Two Imperial Legions have been spotted by the Lion and they come back to the Second City to warn them.]

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until the game ends, Equipping Followers and Items from your hand with a printed Gold Cost of 1 to 4 is a Battle/Open action for you and, after the first time each turn you do so, draw a card.
title: Harbingers of War

flavor: Sukihime's serene mask slipped for a brief moment as she read the missive. "Prepare our defenses," she ordered. "They are coming."
use: Should have something to do with the failed diplomacy between the Colonies and the Empire
designernotes: Otomo Sukihime crumpling up a missive from the empire. Diplomacy has failed and the Imperial Legion amrches.

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, any player who controls the Imperial Favor discards it and, until your second turn from now begins, negate the effects of the first Political action taken each turn.
title: Rejected Mediation

flavor: A sense of bemusement filled the air. "These trifling actions of men have nothing to do with me," the dragon said.
use: Should have something to reflect the fact the Shadow Dragon reveals to the Scorpion that it is not involved in the conflict. As for the title Shaggy, "It wasn't me". Get it? :)
designernotes: Should have something to reflect the fact the Shadow Dragon reveals to the Scorpion that it is not involved in the conflict. As for the title Shaggy, "It wasn't me". Get it? :)

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, show the top five cards of your Dynasty deck. Choose another player who may discard one of the cards, put the rest back in any order, and refill this Province face-up.
title: Revelations

flavor: Neither side wished to let loose the first blood, and the constant rain only served to further dampen the mood.
designernotes: A Monsoon drenching the Second City and those attacking it.

text: Until your next turn begins, after each time a Personality is assigned to attack, negate his straightening during his controller's next Straighten Phase (after he assigns).
title: Unseasonable Weather

flavor: "There is no greater strength, no greater resource, than those who share your bloodline. A family's abilities can be harnessed in war to tremendous effect, and should be." - Akodo's Leadership

text: Limited: Until your next turn begins, your Personalities have +1F while in an army with another Personality with the same Family Name.
notes: Released in the special Imperial Assembly pack in May 2013
erratum: Errata effective upon Ivory Edition release.
title: Family Maneuvers

flavor: Even in the simplest of activities, the madness began to manifest itself among those who seemed most unlikely…
use: [Samurai in the Colonies are going mad and performing their duties in strange and unusual ways.]
designernotes: [Samurai in the Colonies are going mad and performing their duties in strange and unusual ways.]

text: Limited: Until your second turn from now begins, once per turn, you may take a Limited action on a Strategy as an Open action.
title: The Madness Spreads

flavor: With the truth behind the Blood revealed, the other clans sent their finest to petition the Spider for its use.
use: Magistrates meet with Kanpeki and try to convince him to let them use the Blood of the Destroyer
designernotes: Magistrates meet with Kanpeki and try to convince him to let them use the Blood of the Preserver

text: Limited: Search your Fate deck for two attachments with different titles. Show them. Another player chooses one of them and removes it from the game. Put the other attachment in your hand.
title: Dark Audience

flavor: "Commander, how can we know if those outside the gates are corrupted?"
designernotes: Sentries nervously guarding the Second City.

text: Limited: Until your next turn begins, other players' cards will not destroy your Personalities in a defending army.
title: Rampant Paranoia

flavor: In the year 1199 according to the Isawa calendar, the previously unseen influence of the mad dragon, P'an Ku, reached into the hearts of many within the Colonies, and the result was madness and suffering on an undreamt of scale.
use: A riot breaks out in the Second City
designernotes: A riot breaks out in the Second City.

text: Limited: Each player targets and bows an unbowed Samurai.
title: Riot in the Second City

flavor: Doji Hakuseki looked upon the face of the being that had threatened her Empire, and she felt the sin of hate in her heart.
designernotes: Magistrates taking on the Dark Naga.

text: Limited: Target two Unique Personalities in play, exactly one of which is yours. Your Personality challenges the other Personality. Destroy the duel's loser.
title: The Empire's Foe Exposed

flavor: With the news of P'an Ku's involvement, the Governor and the Commander agreed to set aside their conflict in the interest of a greater threat.
use: This represents the temporary truce between Suikihime and the Imperial Commander while they figure out what is going on
designernotes: This represents the temporary truce between Suikihime and the Imperial Commander while they figure out what is going on

text: Limited: Until the game ends, each player draws two cards instead of one at the end of his turn if he put no other cards in his hand that turn.
title: An End to Hostilities

flavor: "There are times in history when madness has inspired incredible victories," Ikoma Natsu said. "How am I to feel about this?"
use: An Event of some point in the past
designernotes: A flashback to Bieden Pass being destroyed to kill the spirit armies.

text: Limited: Until the game ends, this Province does not hold Dynasty cards and has +10 Province Strength.
formattedtitle: The Second Battle of Beiden Pass
title: The Second Battle of Beiden Pass

flavor: Through the haze and the veil of time, the magistrates beheld a terrible sight… a land ruled by fear and madness.
use: An Event prophesizing about the Colonies devolving into complete madness.
designernotes: [Note: This is an event where we see into the future and see that the Colonies have devolved into complete madness and many heroes have been lost. Some of the fallen heroes should be featured in this art showing that they are lost to madness.]

text: After you reveal cards in your Provinces at the start of your turn, if this Event is face-up in one of them, until the game ends, each player has the ability, "Open: Your target Personality with a Madness token commits Seppuku" and any player who ends his turn with 20 or more Madness tokens on his Personalities loses the game.
title: An Empire of Madness

flavor: Punishment to the wicked. Vengeance for the just.
designernotes: http://l5r.wikia.com/wiki/Yozo

text: Limited: Until your second turn from now begins, your Personalities have +1F while attacking a player who declared an attack against you since your last turn ended.
notes: Released to the Level 15, Level 20, and Jewel Event participants
title: Yozo's Inspiration

flavor: Seen through the lessons of Fudo, many things in the Colonies seemed reasonable and righteous. Consequences were ignored in favor of a new freedom.
designernotes: This is the massive confrontation with Fudo

text: Limited: Until your turn begins three turns from now begins, each player may put no more than one Ring into play per turn.
notes: Released in the 2013 Summer Con pack
title: Losing the Way

flavor: "We will restore order!" the commander cried over the shouts of the chaos. "For the Empress. For Rokugan!"
designernotes: The aftermath of a battle leaves all of the Samurai psyched and ready for more fighting.

text: Limited: Permanently give your target Personality Conqueror.
notes: Released in the 2013 Summer Con pack
title: Legionnaire's Appointment

flavor: In the chaos, it was the Phoenix who began to understand what was happening. The Second City and everything near it was engulfed in chaos, but not even the Isawa knew who was gripped by madness and who was simply acting of their free will.
designernotes: The Empire and Colonies realize P'an Ku and Fudo are to blame for the havoc.

text: Limited: Destroy all Madness tokens.
notes: Released in the 2013 Summer Con pack
title: The Fever Breaks

flavor: "I never thought I would want to share a victory with you," Tsudoken said over the cheers. "I never thought I would let you," Yuri replied with a grin.
designernotes: Samurai have defeated their enemies on a field of battle and are celebrating (this is the scene after 10130167)

text: Limited: After each battle resolution (this turn) in which your army destroyed an enemy army or Province, give each of your Personalities in that army a +1F/+1C token.
notes: Released to the 2013 Q4 Organized Play winners
title: A Great Victory

flavor: A celebration of the Fortune of Rice's merciful aspect leads to long celebrations of bounty and generosity.
designernotes: I have no idea what this event should be. I think it would be cool to have this art tied to 10130183 since they are similar cards and both pre-releases.

text: Limited: Give your non-Unique Holdings +1 Gold Production until your next turn begins.
notes: Released to the Gates of Chaos Pre-Release participants
title: Inari's Festival

flavor: The Fortune of Rice's wrathful aspect is not celebrated so much as begged for mercy and a swift departure.
designernotes: I have no idea what this event should be. I think it would be cool to have this art tied to 10130182 since they are similar cards and both pre-releases.

text: Limited: Give all other players' non-Unique Holdings -1 Gold Production until your next turn begins.
notes: Released to the Aftermath Pre-Release participants
title: Frost Dragon Festival

flavor: "The land itself added to the conflict, as loyal samurai and Fallen alike found themselves beset with fever and weakness. True-hearted samurai drew on the strength of honor and forged on. The Fallen had no such resources." - Chronicle of the Heretic War, Part I
designernotes: A samurai, visibly weakened by some tropical malady, fighting against a Fallen.

text:
Personalities and Followers have -1F while attacking.
Limited: Put this Event into play. Discard it after your next turn begins.
title: A Fever in the Blood

flavor: "This is a simple problem. We are going to take back Journey's End City, and anyone who tries to stop us can die beneath our charge." - Shinjo Tselu
designernotes: Fallen forces attacking Journey's End Keep

text: Limited: Defending players may not play Terrains (this turn). After the first time you destroy a Province in battle this turn, if you do not have more Provinces than the Defender (at that time), gain a Province.
title: Journey's End Siege

flavor: "Not content to let the honorless retain Journey's End City, the children of Shinjo assembled their forces and rode to battle. With bushido in their hearts and steel in their hands, they engaged the Fallen." - Chronicle of the Heretic War, Part III
designernotes: Art Note: The Unicorn attack the Fallen at Journey's End Keep.

text: Limited: Your Cavalry Personalities and Followers have +1F while attacking and opposed (this turn).
title: Riding to the Rescue

flavor: "Unfortunate circumstances prevent me from presenting this to your mother, as would be my wish," Kanpeki said with a low bow. "In her stead, naturally, I sought out the most worthy scion of the Imperial families to serve as her representative."
use: In which Kanpeki gifts the Blood of the Preserver to Iweko Shibatsu.
designernotes:
Art: Daigotsu Kanpeki bowing before Iweko Shibatsu, presenting the gift.
Design: The Spider seek glory by turning over the Blood.

text: Limited: Search your hand, then your discard pile, and then your Fate deck for a card titled Blood of the Preserver. Show it. Remove it from the game to gain 2 Honor.
title: Changing the Game

flavor: The Mantis viewed the whole of the Colonies as their domain, regardless of the feelings of the other clans. With certain hidden threats eliminated, the Mantis turned their attention toward threats long forgotten, and began seeking a northern route to distant Medinaat al-Salaam.
use: In which the Mantis begin searching for the northern route to Medinaat al-Salaam.
designernotes:
Art: Mantis Scouts exploring the Northern Wastes.
Design: Intentionally non-Unique.

title: Into the Wastes
flavor: "It matters not what the others think," Yoritomo Hiromi said indignantly. "The Colonies are Mantis territory and I wish to know what lies beyond our borders. If there is a threat, we must know."
text: Engage: You have the first opportunity in this battle to pass or take a Battle action, which must come from a card in a unit.
designernotes:
Art: Mantis Scouts exploring the Northern Wastes.
Design: Intentionally non-Unique.

text: Engage: You have the first opportunity in this battle to pass or take a Battle action, which must come from a card in a unit.
title: Into the Wastes

flavor: The wedding of an individual like the Emerald Champion is an occasion of rare gravity indeed. The intended's family gains incredible wealth and renown, and typically hosts a celebration of enormous scope.
designernotes: An extravagant wedding.

text: Limited: Put this Event into play. Target your Personality. While this Event remains in play, he will not be dishonored and you will not lose Honor from other players' actions that have targeted him . After your second turn from now begins, discard this Event.
title: Marriage of the Emerald Champion

flavor: Throughout the Colonies, the monasteries and hidden strongholds of the Fudo cult were sought out and put to the torch. Those who had embraced the blasphemous philosophy either recanted and were taken into custody, or refused, and were slain outright.
use: Imperial Legions hunting the Fudo cultists
designernotes: Art Note: This is the Imperial Legions hunting the Fudo cultists

text: Limited: Starting with you, each player targets (if possible) and destroys a card with the printed Fallen, Fudo, or Shadowlands keyword.
title: Purge of Fudoism

text:
After a player gains Honor, or any of his opponents lose Honor from the rulebook or cards they do not own, give him an equal number of Influence tokens. A player who begins his turn with 50 or more Influence tokens wins the game. This Event will not be discarded from play, nor have its effects prevented.
Political Open: If no player controls a copy of Political Standoff, put this Event into play.
erratum: Tech errata to clarify the scope of its prevention trait, via Oracle.
title: Political Standoff

flavor: Though it has been long months since she stepped down, the abdication of the Jade Champion remains a matter of brisk discussion.
use: This is the forced Retirement of the Jade Champion
designernotes: Asahina Nanae resignating from the Jade Champion position, looking both angry and ashamed.

text:
Reduce all Honor gains and losses from Holdings by 1 (minimum zero).
Open: Put this Event into play. Discard it before your next turn ends. You may target and dishonor a Crane Clan Personality.
title: Abdication

flavor: Although the two sons of the Divine Empress did not sanction action against one another, their followers were happy to take that initiative on their own.
use: An Event that interacts with the "Support" cards below, should deal with violence breaking out in the Second City and clans picking a side
designernotes: When violence breaks out in the Second City, samurai have to choose whom they support

text:
Political Open: Search your Fate deck for an Allegiance card, show it, and put it in your hand.
Political Open: Discard a target Allegiance card in play. Refill this Province face-up.
title: The Samurai Caste Divides

flavor: The forces of the Dark Naga, mistakenly believed dead, emerged from the wilderness to lay siege to the Second City. The blood of man and Naga alike flooded the streets.
use: The Dark Naga wages war on the Colonies
designernotes: Art Notes - The Dark Naga and the Shakash leading a massive army of naga from the seas to assault a waiting army of samurai. Should feel epic and desperate.

text: Open: Permanently give your target Personality Resilient. If you have fewer Provinces than each other player, permanently give him Conqueror and refill this Province face-up.
title: The Serpent War

flavor: The arrival of an Imperial heir is cause for circumstance for every city.
designernotes: Artwork has Seiken disembarking from a boat.

text: Political Battle/Open: Take the Imperial Favor.
title: Auspicious Arrival

flavor: "It does not matter if you think you do not play the game," she said, smiling. "Everyone plays. Those who claim to avoid it simply lose."
use: This should have something to do with Seiken's attitude towards the Governess
designernotes: Seiken rebuking the Governess.

text:
After a player's Favor action resolves, he loses 2 Honor.
Open: Put this Event into play. Discard it after your second turn from now begins.
title: Denial

flavor: The Emperor's Blessing will help the needy rebuild their homes, restoring them to their former glory.
use: This should do something with Iweko being presented with the Blood of the Preserver
designernotes: Notes: Shows the positive effects that the Blood has on the earth, as mentioned on the Kotei pack cards. It's bountiful.

text: Economic Open: Target your Holding. After your next turn begins, give it a +1 Gold Production Wealth token.
title: The Blessing

flavor: The military force of the traditionalists was vastly larger than their opponents', and a swift victory was anticipated. They reckoned without the duplicity of the Scorpion, however.
designernotes: [Note: The traditionalists suffer a rout when the Scorpion betray them.]

text: Interrupt: You may choose a different one of your (legal) Personalities for the action to target instead of the one chosen by another player, now or after he chooses the target.
title: Devastating Betrayal

flavor: Throughout Rokugan's history, there have been occasions when Emperors have retired to their personal chambers for an extensive period of time in order to contemplate matters of great importance.
designernotes: Notes - Seclusion and protection. Iweko alone at a shrine meditating. This is Iweko going into seclusion to determine who the heir should be.

text: Open: Remove your target Personality from the game. Search your Dynasty deck for a Personality with equal or lower Gold Cost and refill this Province with him face-up.
title: Seclusion

flavor: The certainty of the Heavens rained down upon the mortal mind of a man named Enomoto, and he was shattered by it. Far away, the aged monk Hira felt something stir within him. "What… what is this I see?" he wondered idly. "What is happening?"
designernotes: Asahina Hira knows something just happened to the world. Enomoto gets crazy visions, writes them down, then dies. Not sure which should be depicted here, maybe both? Either way, this is about the same Prophecy

text:
You have the ability, "Open: Give a target face-up card in your Province Kharmic."
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: A Vision Imparted

flavor: The season of festivals ended finally in a week of merriment before the coronation of Iweko II.
designernotes: [Note: This is the end of the 90 days of Festivals. I figure this could be the last one, signaling the end. Makes it so being down on Provinces does not also mean you are down on the number of cards you can buy.]

text:
You have the ability, "Open: If you have fewer Provinces than one other player, then a number of times per turn equal to the difference (now), you may refill one of your Provinces face-up."
Open: If you have not resolved a Coronation Festival this game, put this Event into play.
title: Coronation Festival

flavor: Kanpeki looked at the burning banner, then back to his men. "We have kept our oaths, and they have broken theirs. You know, as I know, what this means. I will not have my father's name slandered. The Empire must burn."
designernotes: Art - Kanpeki and the Spider Clan Court delegates look on as the Spider Clan is disbanded by Imperial Decree.

text:
You have the ability, "Battle: If your current army is opposed, discard a card to draw a card."
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Exodus of the Spider

flavor: The Spider's unholy army outnumbered the temple's defenders more than fifty to one. The samurai fought with honor, but they could not hold back the dark tide. Renyu and Norimichi's magic tore the world asunder, but when Norimichi fell, their defeat was inevitable.
designernotes: [Art Note: The First Seal is destroyed.]

text: Open: Dark times are coming. Personalities with 3 or more Personal Honor have -1F. Personalities with 0 Personal Honor have +1F.
title: Battle of the First Seal

flavor: The crowd fell silent as Kozan continued. "The Dark Lord has chosen to renege on his agreement with the Empress. The Taint endures… it has only remained hidden."
designernotes: Art: Hida Kozan (Voice of the Emperor) reading an official decree in the Imperial Court. The surrounding courtiers look confused and angry. This is Kozan letting people know that Iweko has been suppressing the taint.

text:
Invest :g2:: The Event may be Unique.
Political Open: Search your Dynasty deck for a non-Unique Event not named Ominous Revelation and refill this Province with it, face-up.
title: Ominous Revelation

flavor: As the ancestral guardians shimmered into existence, the samurai-ko licked her lips anxiously. "This was not in the intelligence," she whispered. Then the air shimmered again, and the hungry dead spilled forth. "The Kwanchai's daughter will not be harmed!" they hissed.
designernotes: Disguised as Mantis, a force of Scorpion ninja infiltrate the libraries of Kyuden Miya and Shiro Ikoma to remove any hint of the Three Seals that they might find, possibly planting false evidence in the process. This is an attempt to stop the prophecy from coming to be fulfilled. While the heist at Kyuden Miya goes off without a hitch, the one in the Lion lands does not. The Scorpion are able to deftly avoid the Lion's attention, but the Kitsu have employed the aide of their ancestral spirits to protect major Lion fortifications during the war, and the spirits raise the alarm. When this happens, the leader of the team and the greatest ninja of the Scorpion (also Bayushi Kwanchai's daughter) inadvertently sets off her own legacy and the spirits of gaki-do rush to her defense. Shiro Ikoma quickly becomes the site of a battle between the spirit armies of the blessed ancestors and the hungry dead, giving the Scorpion time to escape and confusing the Lion to no end.

text: Ninja Open, :g2:: You may target and bow a Spell. Search your Fate deck for a Ninja Strategy, show it, and put it in your hand. Lose 2 Honor.
title: War of the Spirit Realms

flavor: "Enough of death. Enough of war. Let there be peace at last." - Toturi, the Black Lion

text:
This Event may not have its effects negated or be discarded from play. After a turn begins, if the active player controls seven Seven Thunder Personalities with no Clan Alignments in common, he or she wins the game.
Open: Put this Event into play.
title: Victory of the Thunders
